title: Game of Darts Guide
seoTitle: Complete Game of Darts Guide | BarkinMad Studios
description: Complete Game of Darts guide covering setup, rules, scoring and features.
summary: Everything you need to know about Game of Darts in one guide.

MARK: - Privacy Policy Section
\

struct
 PrivacyPolicySection: View \\
 \
 @EnvironmentObject 
var
 orientationManager: OrientationManager\
 \

var
 body: 
some
 View \\
 HelpCard(title: "Privacy Policy", systemImage: "shield") \\
 Text("""\
 This app collects minimal data to support gameplay and display advertisements.\
 We use Google AdMob to serve ads. Google may collect device information such as advertising identifiers (e.g., IDFA), usage data, and other anonymous metrics to display relevant ads.\
 \
 You can choose whether to allow tracking when prompted at launch. This is used only for ad personalization.\
 Game stats and your custom player name are stored locally on your device and never shared.\
 \
 For more information on how Google uses your data, visit:\
 \
 https://policies.google.com/technologies/partner-sites\
 """)\
 .font(.subheadline)\
 .padding()\
 \\
 \\
\\

 body: 
some
 View \\
 HelpCard(title: "About Us", systemImage: "info.circle") \\
 Text("""\
 Innovative Apps Crafted with Passion\
 \
 At BarkinMad Studios, we believe in creating engaging, user-friendly, and high-quality apps that bring value and fun to your fingertips. Our mission is to combine creativity with cutting-edge technology to deliver seamless experiences for users of all kinds.\
 \
 Our latest app is designed with precision and care, focusing on intuitive design, smooth performance, and features that users actually want. Whether it\'92s productivity, entertainment, or lifestyle, BarkinMad Studios aims to set new standards in mobile innovation.\
 """)\
 .font(.subheadline)\
 .padding()\
 \\

let
 rulesData: [RuleItem] = [\
 .init(title: "Match Darts", key: "match options"),\
 .init(title: "Cricket", key: "cricket options"),\
 .init(title: "Snooker", key: "snooker options"),\
 .init(title: "Bob\'92s 27", key: "bobs27 options"),\
 .init(title: "1 to 20", key: "1 to 20 options"),\
 .init(title: "Hare & Hound", key: "hare & hound options"),\
 .init(title: "Halve It", key: "halve it options"),\
 .init(title: "All 5\'92s", key: "all 5s options")\
 ]\

var
 body: 
some
 View \\
 VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 12) \\
 \
 // Custom search field wrapper\
 TextField("Search rules", text: $searchText)\
 .padding(8)\
 .background(AppColor.chalkWhite)\
 .cornerRadius(8)\
 .shadow(color: .primary.opacity(0.1), radius: 2, x: 0, y: 1)\
 \
 \
 ScrollView \\
 VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 12) \\
 ForEach(filteredRules) \ rule 
in
\
 DisclosureGroup \\
 Text(rules(for: rule.key))\
 .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading)\
 \ label: \\
 Text(rule.title)\
 .font(AppFontStyle.style6.chalkduster())\
 .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading)\
 \\
 .animation(.easeInOut, value: rules(for: rule.key))\
 .padding(.vertical, 3)\
 .padding(.horizontal)\
 .cardStyle()\
 \\
 \

if
 filteredRules.isEmpty \\
 Text("No rules found.")\
 .font(AppFontStyle.style5.chalkduster())\
 \\
 \\
 \\
 \\
 .navigationTitle("Game Rules")\
 .background(.clear)\
 \\

import
 SwiftUI\
\
// 
MARK: - Rules for Games
\
// Helper function to map titleName to the rules\

return
 "Rules not available for this game."\
 \\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 Start with 501/301/701 points.\
 \'95 Subtract each turn's score from your total until you reach exactly 0.\
 \'95 You must finish on a double or the bullseye.\
 \'95 You bust if you go below 0 or leave a score of 1.\
 \
 Legs & Sets\
 \'95 A leg is one game of 501, 301, or 701.\
 \'95 A set is the first to win a specified number of legs.\
 \'95 Common in tournament play (bestof formats).\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 Objective: Hit numbers 1 through 20 in order.\
 \'95 Each turn consists of 3 darts.\
 \'95 First to complete all numbers wins. Track hit rate to compare performance.\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 Starting score: 27 points per player.\
 \'95 Objective: Hit doubles in order from Double 1 to Double 20, then the bullseye.\
 \
 Gameplay\
 \'95 Each turn, throw 3 darts at the designated double for that round.\
 \'95 Each dart that hits the target double scores its double value (e.g., Double 1 = 2 points, Double 20 = 40 points).\
 \'95 If you miss the target double with all 3 darts, subtract the double's value from your total.\
 \
 Scoring examples\
 \'95 Aiming for Double 1 (worth 2 points):\
 \'96 Hitting 2 darts: +4 points (2 \'d7 2)\
 \'96 Hitting 1 dart: +2 points\
 \'96 Missing all 3 darts: 2 points\
 \
 Winning the game\
 \'95 Continue through Double 20 and finish on the bullseye.\
 \'95 If a player's score drops to 0 or below at any time, that player is eliminated.\
 \
 Maximum score\
 \'95 A perfect game\'97hitting all 3 darts on each double and the bullseye\'97yields a maximum of 1,437 points.\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 Twoplayer game.\
 \'95 Hare starts on 1; Hound starts on 14.\
 \
 \'95 When a player hits their current target, they advance to the next required segment.\
 \'95 The Hare wins by reaching 20 before the Hound catches them.\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 Numbers used: 15\'9620 and the bullseye.\
 \'95 Each number must be hit 3 times (Single = 1, Double = 2, Triple = 3).\
 \'95 Once a number is closed, you may score on it until your opponent also closes it.\
 \'95 Winner: closes all numbers and the bullseye and has the higher score.\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 Each player is assigned a random number.\
 \'95 Hit your double to become a \'93killer.\'94\
 \'95 Killers can hit opponents\'92 numbers to reduce their lives.\
 \'95 Last player with lives remaining wins.\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 9 innings (rounds).\
 \'95 In inning N, only hits on number N score (Single = 1 run, Double = 2, Triple = 3).\
 \'95 Highest total runs after 9 innings wins.\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 Target numbers are chosen before the game (e.g., 20, 16, Double 7, bullseye).\
 \'95 You score only by hitting the target(s) during your turn.\
 \'95 If you miss with all 3 darts in a round, your score is halved (rounded down).\
 \'95 Highest score at the end wins.\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 Each round focuses on a single number (Round 1 = 1s, Round 2 = 2s, \'85).\
 \'95 Points equal the value of hits on that round\'92s number (Single, Double, Triple).\
 \'95 Automatic win if you hit a single, double, and triple of the round\'92s number in the same turn (a \'93Shanghai\'94).\
 \'95 Game ends after 20 rounds; highest score wins.\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 Each turn, throw three darts and total the score.\
 \'95 If the total is divisible by 5, you score total \'f7 5 points (whole numbers only).\
 \'95 First to reach exactly 51 points wins.\
 \'95 If you exceed 51, you bust and your score reverts to what it was before that turn.\
 \
 Tips & strategy\
 \'95 Aim for neighboring segments divisible by 5 (e.g., 20/5 or 10/15) to increase your chances while reducing risk.\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 Snooker darts mirrors traditional snooker scoring. Each board segment represents a ball, and closing a number equates to potting that ball. The aim is to close all numbers (balls) and finish with a higher score than your opponent.\
 \
 \'95 To determine who starts, each player throws at the bullseye; closest begins.\
 \'95 The starting player must first hit a red (numbers 1\'9615). Each red scores 1 point.\
 \'95 After a red, aim for a colour (16\'9620 or bullseye). Declare the intended colour before throwing.\
 \
 Colour values\
 \'95 1\'9615: Reds \'97 1 point each\
 \'95 16: Yellow \'97 2 points\
 \'95 17: Green \'97 3 points\
 \'95 18: Brown \'97 4 points\
 \'95 19: Blue \'97 5 points\
 \'95 20: Pink \'97 6 points\
 \'95 Bullseye: Black \'97 7 points\
 """\
\\
\

return
 """\
 \'95 Typical format: 9 or 18 holes (rounds). Each hole has a designated target number.\
 \'95 On each hole, players throw 3 darts at that hole\'92s target. Lowest number of darts required to hit the target scores best (par concept varies by variant).\
 \'95 Common variant: Score the fewest total darts to hit the target on each hole; missing entirely scores a max (e.g., 5 or 6).\
 \'95 After all holes, the lowest total wins.\
 """\
\\
\
